narrative.headline: 2018 Hyundai Sonata: the engine dominates owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 361 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is by far the most reported area at 56%, more than three times the next area, the electrical system (17%), followed by the power train (11%). No deaths are reported. The engine complaints also carry the most fires (7), so it is the clear area to check closely.

issues.sample: PURCHASED NEW 2018 SONATA SEL MODEL IN CALIFORNIA. WHILE DRIVING, THE TRANSMISSION STARTS TO HARD DOWNSHIFT WHEN THE BRAKE IS APPLIED.  I FOUND OUT THAT THIS HARD DOWNSHIFTING AND JOLTING/THUMPING OF ENGINE AND TRANSMISSION ONLY OCCURS WHEN COMING TO A STOP AT SPEEDS BETWEEN 10 T
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The dominant complaint by far: 203 reports (56%), with 7 fires, 3 crashes, and 2 injuries. The high fire count makes this the area to inspect most carefully.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 60 reports (17%), including 4 fires, 3 crashes, and 2 injuries. Worth a careful check given the fire count.

issues.sample: THROTTLE RESPONSE: FEELS LIKE THE OLD VEHICLES WITH A FLAT SPOT IN THE CARBURETOR ACCELERATOR PUMP.  THE "THREE SECOND' RULE APPLIES WHEN THE THROTTLE PETAL IS PUSHED. THERE IS A SERIOUS HESITATION AS THE ENGINE TENDS TO FALL FLAT BEFORE ACCELERATING. HAS TO DUE WITH THE SIX SPEE
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 39 reports (11%), tied to 2 crashes, 1 fire, and 1 injury. Owners describe hesitation when accelerating, so test the throttle response on a drive.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 29 reports (8%), with 1 fire and no crashes or injuries. A smaller but recurring complaint area.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: The fewest of the top five: 22 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners cite headlight issues like condensation.
